page.primary_keyword: Puerto Banus yacht charter cost per person
page.title: Puerto Banus Yacht Charter Cost Per Person: A Complete Breakdown

data.meta_description: Discover the real Puerto Banus yacht charter cost per person. From €749 for 9 guests to €4,719 for 12 with jet ski. Full breakdown inside.
data.summary: A transparent breakdown of Puerto Banus yacht charter cost per person, with real prices from our fleet. Learn how to split costs fairly, what's included, and which boat suits your group size.
key_facts: Astondoa 40: €749/2h for up to 9 guests – €83/person for 9 sharing
Azimut 39: €749/2h for up to 11 guests – €68/person for 11 sharing
Mangusta 80 (all variants): €4,719 minimum 4h for up to 12 guests – includes a free jet ski
All prices include licensed skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, insurance, and 21% IVA
Longer charters cost less per hour: e.g. Astondoa 40 at €2,299/8h = €287/hour
Other boats (e.g. Ferretti 94, Canados 86) – pricing on request via WhatsApp
All boats depart from Puerto Banús – no hidden port fees
Skipper included – no extra crew cost to split

faq.q: What is the cheapest Puerto Banus yacht charter cost per person?
faq.a: The lowest per-person cost is on the Azimut 39 (11 guests) at €749 for 2 hours – that's about €68 per person. For a full day (8 hours), the Astondoa 40 at €2,299 split 9 ways works out at €255 per person.

faq.q: Are drinks and food included in the per-person cost?
faq.a: Yes. Every charter includes water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, and light snacks. No extra per-person charge for these – they're part of the flat boat price.

faq.q: How many people can I split the cost with?
faq.a: Maximum capacity varies: Astondoa 40: 9 pax, Azimut 39: 11 pax, Mangusta 80: 12 pax. You can split the total cost among the group however you like – we charge per boat, not per person.

faq.q: Is the skipper included in the per-person cost?
faq.a: Absolutely. A licensed skipper is included in every charter price. You don't need to tip separately or hire a captain – it's all part of the flat rate.

faq.q: Do I pay extra for fuel or insurance?
faq.a: No. Fuel, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21%) are all included in the quoted price. There are no hidden costs to split among the group.

faq.q: Can I charter a larger yacht like the Ferretti 94 per person?
faq.a: Yes – pricing for the Ferretti 94, Canados 86, and other superyachts is on request via WhatsApp. Contact us with your group size and preferred date for a custom quote.

faq.q: How do I book and calculate the final per-person cost?
faq.a: Choose your boat and duration, then divide the total price by your group size. Book via our website or WhatsApp. For example: Azimut 39 for 4 hours (€1,199) ÷ 11 people = €109 per person.

faq.q: What's the best value boat for a large group?
faq.a: The Mangusta 80 (24m, 12 pax) at €4,719 for 4 hours includes a free jet ski – that's €393 per person for 12 guests. For a full day, the Azimut 58 Flybridge (12 pax) is priced on request.

data.body_html:
Understanding the Puerto Banus yacht charter cost per person is the first step to planning a stress-free day on the water. Unlike many holiday expenses, boat hire in Marbella is charged per vessel, not per head – so the more friends you bring, the less each person pays. In this complete breakdown, we'll show you real prices from our fleet, what's included, and how to split costs fairly.

How Puerto Banus Yacht Charter Pricing Works

Every charter from Boat Rental Marbella is a flat rate for the boat, not per person. You pay one price for the vessel, skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, insurance, and tax. That total cost is then divided among your group. For example, an Astondoa 40 for 2 hours costs €749 – split among 9 guests, that's just €83 per person.

Real Per-Person Costs for Our Most Popular Boats

Here's how the numbers stack up for our most-booked vessels. All prices include skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, insurance, and 21% IVA.

  • Astondoa 40 (12.5m, 9 pax): €749/2h → €83/person (9 sharing); €2,299/8h → €255/person
  • Azimut 39 (12.5m, 11 pax): €749/2h → €68/person (11 sharing); €2,299/8h → €209/person
  • Mangusta 80 (24m, 12 pax): €4,719/4h → €393/person (12 sharing) – includes free jet ski
  • Mangusta 80 (White) (24m, 12 pax): same pricing as above
  • Mangusta 80 (Grey) (24m, 12 pax): same pricing as above

For other boats like the Ferretti 94, Canados 86, or Azimut 58 Flybridge, pricing is on request via WhatsApp – contact us with your group size for a custom per-person quote.

What's Included in the Per-Person Cost?

Every charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21%). There are no hidden fees – the price you see is the total price. You don't need to bring anything except sunscreen and a towel.

How to Calculate Your Own Per-Person Cost

Choose your boat and duration from our fleet page, then divide the total by your group size. For example:

  • Azimut 39 for 4 hours: €1,199 ÷ 11 guests = €109 per person
  • Astondoa 40 for 6 hours: €1,899 ÷ 9 guests = €211 per person
  • Mangusta 80 for 4 hours: €4,719 ÷ 12 guests = €393 per person (with jet ski included)

Longer charters offer better value per hour. The Astondoa 40 at €2,299 for 8 hours works out at €287/hour – cheaper per hour than the 2-hour rate of €374/hour.

Why Per-Person Charter Costs Are Lower Than You Think

Compared to a night out on the Golden Mile or a dinner at a Puerto Banús beach club, a yacht charter split among friends is often more affordable. For €68 per person, you get 2 hours of private cruising with drinks and snacks included – no cover charges, no minimum spend. Plus, you control the itinerary: anchor at Cala del Faro, swim off Cabopino, or cruise past La Concha.

Larger Groups: Superyacht Pricing on Request

For groups of 12 or more, our superyachts like the Ferretti 94 (29m, 12 pax), Canados 86 (26m, 12 pax), and Maiora 26m (26m, 12 pax) offer luxury at a competitive per-person rate when you fill the boat. Contact us via WhatsApp for a custom quote – we'll break down the per-person cost for your specific date.

Tips for Keeping Your Per-Person Cost Low

  • Fill the boat to capacity – the more guests, the lower each person pays
  • Choose longer durations – 8-hour charters have the best hourly rates
  • Book on weekdays – availability is better, though prices are fixed
  • Skip the extras – our standard package already includes drinks and snacks

Ready to Book? Here's How

Browse our fleet, pick your boat and duration, then divide the price by your group size. Book directly online or via WhatsApp. All charters depart from Puerto Banús – we'll meet you at the marina. For any questions about per-person costs or group sizes, get in touch.

Frequently Asked Questions

What is the cheapest Puerto Banus yacht charter cost per person?

The lowest per-person cost is on the Azimut 39 (11 guests) at €749 for 2 hours – that's about €68 per person. For a full day (8 hours), the Astondoa 40 at €2,299 split 9 ways works out at €255 per person.

Are drinks and food included in the per-person cost?

Yes. Every charter includes water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, and light snacks. No extra per-person charge for these – they're part of the flat boat price.

How many people can I split the cost with?

Maximum capacity varies: Astondoa 40: 9 pax, Azimut 39: 11 pax, Mangusta 80: 12 pax. You can split the total cost among the group however you like – we charge per boat, not per person.

Is the skipper included in the per-person cost?

Absolutely. A licensed skipper is included in every charter price. You don't need to tip separately or hire a captain – it's all part of the flat rate.

Do I pay extra for fuel or insurance?

No. Fuel, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21%) are all included in the quoted price. There are no hidden costs to split among the group.

Can I charter a larger yacht like the Ferretti 94 per person?

Yes – pricing for the Ferretti 94, Canados 86, and other superyachts is on request via WhatsApp. Contact us with your group size and preferred date for a custom quote.

How do I book and calculate the final per-person cost?

Choose your boat and duration, then divide the total price by your group size. Book via our website or WhatsApp. For example: Azimut 39 for 4 hours (€1,199) ÷ 11 people = €109 per person.

What's the best value boat for a large group?

The Mangusta 80 (24m, 12 pax) at €4,719 for 4 hours includes a free jet ski – that's €393 per person for 12 guests. For a full day, the Azimut 58 Flybridge (12 pax) is priced on request.
